steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has 'The powerful blast' as the subject and 'ejected particles' later — where 'ejected' must serve as the main verb. The blank determines whether the verb form after the subject creates an independent clause or a participial modifier.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — results: finite present-tense verb — creates an independent clause ('The powerful blast results from...'), leaving 'ejected particles' unattached. ✗
  • Choice B — resulting: present participle — turns the phrase into a modifier ('The powerful blast resulting from...'), so 'ejected' can serve as the main verb. ✓
  • Choice C — had resulted: finite past-tense verb — same problem as A; creates a complete independent clause and orphans 'ejected particles.' ✗
  • Choice D — resulted: finite past-perfect verb — same structural problem as A and C. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
